t_ch: 怎么是这两位啊？！
t_en: Huh? Why are these two here!?

t_ch: 不出所料…
t_en: As I expected...

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，好久不见。
t_en: It's been a while, Aglaea.

t_ch: 何出此言？明明几分钟前，我们还在一起。
t_en: Whatever do you mean? It was just a few minutes ago that we were together.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}来了兴致，想和大家玩「新朋友游戏」呢。嘻，就配合{F#她}{M#他}一下吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME} seems excited and wants to play the "New Friend Game" with everyone. Hehe, let's humor {M#him}{F#her}.

t_ch: 应该不用我多介绍。阿格莱雅和刻律德菈，她们位高权重，在团队管理方面很有心得。
t_en: I believe they need no further introduction. Aglaea and Cerydra are prominent figures, with great expertise in managing teams.

t_ch: 早听闻昔涟找了位合适的店长，这次得见，确有几分名士风采。
t_en: Word had reached me that Cyrene found an excellent manager. Seeing you today, I must say... you do have a noble bearing.

t_ch: 我——奥赫玛同盟的领袖，圣城的凯撒——将为你执掌{RUBY_B#店长助理}「餐馆主管」{RUBY_E#}一职。若对经营有所疑问，尽可来此问询！
t_en: I, leader of the Okhema Alliance, Imperator of the holy city, shall serve as your {RUBY_B#Manager's Assistant}"Restaurant Supervisor"{RUBY_E#}. Should you have any questions about management, feel free to come and ask!

t_ch: 至于团队建设…我提前做了些安排，算算时间，那两位也该回来了。
t_en: As for assembling the team... I planned ahead. If my timing is right, those two should be back any moment now.

t_ch: 说到就到！*我们*找到好多愿意当服务员的妖精啦，缇安正在给它们做入职培训。
t_en: Right on cue! We found lots of fairies willing to be waitstaff, and Trianne's onboarding them as we speak!

t_ch: 试运营的菜单也准备好了。放心，都是我的拿手菜，保证没问题！
t_en: The trial menu is ready too! Don't worry. These are all my best dishes. There won't be any issues!

t_ch: 伙伴越来越多了。
t_en: The team just keeps getting bigger.

t_ch: 这两位的设定也在意料之中…
t_en: Those two... exactly what I expected.

t_ch: 缇宝、缇宁和缇安，可靠的送餐服务负责人，也是员工的好老师。
t_en: Tribbie, Trinnon, Trianne. Responsible for our delivery service, and also great mentors for the team.

t_ch: 还有白厄，咱们的好朋友，自称做得一手好菜，现在是餐馆主厨（临时）。
t_en: And of course, Phainon, our good friend who insists he's a fantastic cook. He's acting as the restaurant's (provisional) head chef.

t_ch: 什么零食？我做的都是正经菜。不信的话，当场就给你们露几手。
t_en: Think I can only rustle up provisions? No way. I make proper, serious dishes. If you don't believe me, I'll show you what I can do, right here!

t_ch: 服务员妖精们也跃跃欲试。择时不如撞时，这就检验一下大家的训练成果吧。
t_en: The fairy waitstaff are all itching to start. No point in delaying. Time to test the results of everyone's training!

t_ch: 比预想中更顺利呢。既然大家都准备好了……
t_en: Things are going even smoother than I expected. Since everyone's ready...

t_ch: 那就立马开始第一次营业吧。看你了，店长。
t_en: Alright, let's start the first day of operations. It's all up to you now, Manager.

t_ch: 餐馆店长，正式上任！
t_en: Restaurant Manager, reporting for duty!

t_ch: 迷！好、好好吃！大家都开心，喜欢！谢谢你们！
t_en: Mem! So yummy! Everyone's delighted. We really love it! Thank you!

t_ch: 我宣布——妖精餐馆首次经营，完美结束！
t_en: I hereby declare the fairy restaurant's very first day of business has come to a perfect close!

t_ch: 当然，对你来说一定轻轻松松，对吧？不打算说点什么吗？
t_en: Of course, it must've been a breeze for you, right? Not going to say a few words?

t_ch: 感谢各位的辛苦付出。
t_en: Thank you all for your hard work and dedication.

t_ch: 好正经呀！莫非…{NICKNAME}其实有领导者的潜能？
t_en: So formal! Perhaps... {NICKNAME} might just have some leadership potential?

t_ch: 不了，我为人低调。
t_en: No thanks. I like to keep a low profile.

t_ch: 嘻，那我帮你说：感谢大家的辛苦付出，尤其要感谢我们尊敬的店长哦！
t_en: Hehe, then I'll say it for you: Thank you everyone for your hard work. Most of all, our honorable Manager!

t_ch: （鼓掌声）
t_en: (Applaud)

t_ch: 不过，想要为妖精们提供更丰富的菜品，目前的食材种类恐怕远远不够。
t_en: However, if we want to offer the fairies a wider variety of dishes, our current range of ingredients is still quite lacking.

t_ch: 确实呢，要是菜单总不更新，客人们很快也会吃腻的。
t_en: Absolutely. If we don't keep updating the menu, the guests will get tired of the same old dishes.

t_ch: 这点不必担心。我早有预料，特地聘请了两位农林专家。
t_en: That won't be a problem. I foresaw this and hired two experts in agriculture and forestry.

t_ch: 虽说其中一位…费了我不少工夫，但最终还是解决了。他们这会儿应该在农田。
t_en: Though I must say, one of them... took quite a bit of effort to convince. But in the end, it all worked out. They should be at the farm right now.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，真可靠呀！那我们这就去看看专家的成果吧。
t_en: Aglaea you're amazing as always! Alright, let's go take a look at the experts' work.

t_ch: 把种子撒进土里，安静等它发芽，最后结出沉甸甸的果实…收获真是件开心事呀。
t_en: Scatter seeds into the soil, wait quietly for their first green shoots, and in the end, they'll bear heavy, ripe fruit... There's nothing quite as joyful as a harvest.

t_ch: （…好吧，见到什么人都不奇怪。我完全接受这个设定了。）
t_en: (...Alright, whoever appears now, I won't question it. I've completely embraced this world's logic.)

t_ch: 那刻夏老师，缺少光照的问题真能解决吗？
t_en: Professor Anaxa, can you truly solve the limited sunlight problem?

t_ch: 叫我阿那克萨戈拉斯，风堇。即便在游学期间，也不要忘了课堂的规矩。
t_en: Refer to me as Anaxagoras, Hyacine. A field trip is no excuse to disregard classroom discipline.

t_ch: 竟然是…七贤人那刻夏，还有昏光庭院的首席，风堇小姐！
t_en: It's really them... Anaxa of the Seven Sages and Hyacine of the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 首先，叫我阿那克萨戈拉斯。
t_en: Firstly, call me Anaxagoras.

t_ch: 其次，我对招呼小动物吃饭没兴趣，杂务会由我的助教代为完成。
t_en: Secondly, I have no interest in feeding small creatures. My assistant will take care of trivial chores.

t_ch: 别看老师反应这么冷淡，刚到这里的时候，他可是最激动的那个。
t_en: Don't let the professor's cold demeanor fool you. When we first arrived, he was the most excited of all!

t_ch: 一边念叨着：「这是…炼金术的奇迹！」，一边就扑向了那群小家伙！哎，当时我都担心老师会把妖精们丢进法阵里呢。
t_en: He was going on and on: "This is... a miracle of alchemy!" And then dove right toward those tiny critters! Honestly, I was worried he'd toss the fairies into a matrix right then and there.

t_ch: 倒不会做到那种程度，至多取些样本用作研究罢了。
t_en: I wouldn't go that far. At most, I'd take a few samples for research purposes.

t_ch: 那应该也不行吧……
t_en: That's... probably not allowed either...

t_ch: 呀，光顾着聊天，可不能忘了正事呀。
t_en: Ah, I was talking so much that I almost forgot what you came here for.

t_ch: 灰宝是想了解农田的情况吧？老师才刚培育出适应当地环境的作物，感兴趣的话，可以先播种试试看哦。
t_en: Grayie, you're curious about the farmland, right? The professor just finished developing some crops perfect for the local environment. If you're interested, you can try planting some yourself!

t_ch: 快让我在迷境种地！
t_en: Let me farm in the Maze already!

t_ch: 这样就都种上啦。等待一天时间，田地里的作物就会成熟，记得要按时来收获哦。
t_en: All done! Now we just wait a day, and the field will be full of ripe crops. Don't forget to harvest on time!

t_ch: 长得这么快？
t_en: They grow that fast?

t_ch: 不用进行光合作用吗？
t_en: Don't they need to perform photosynthesis?

t_ch: 迷路迷境的生态也太神奇了吧！
t_en: The ecology of Membrance Maze is just amazing!

t_ch: 毕竟两位是理性学府的专家嘛，解决这种小问题应该不在话下。
t_en: Given that these two are experts from the Academy of Reason, something this trivial should be no trouble at all for them.

t_ch: 好啦，从来到迷境开始，一连串经历了好多事，有些累了呢。
t_en: Phew, ever since we arrived in the Maze, it's been one thing after another... I'm feeling a little worn out.

t_ch: 我也有点困困的，该准备休息啦。
t_en: I'm starting to feel sleepy too... Guess it's time to wind down.

t_ch: 约好了，明天还在这里见哦。
t_en: Alright, we'll meet here again tomorrow!

t_ch: 小灰！快点快点，你看，小妖精们早早就在餐馆门口排队啦。
t_en: Little Gray! Hurry up! Look, the fairies are already lining up outside the restaurant!

t_ch: 迷！怎么还不开门，等得毛都要秃了迷！
t_en: Mem! Why isn't it open yet? I'm growing fur-roots here!

t_ch: 没想到试营业反响这么好。农田里的作物应该也都成熟了，来大干一场吧，伙伴。
t_en: Can't believe our soft launch is already such a hit. The crops in the field should be ready too. Now it's time to go all out, partner.

t_ch: 看来我们的主厨跃跃欲试呢。不如现在就开始营业吧？可别让小妖精们久等了呀。
t_en: Looks like our head chef can't wait to get started. Why don't we open for business now? Let's not keep the fairies waiting.

t_ch: 嗯，开始吧。
t_en: Alright, let's do it.

t_ch: 不过，开启今日的营业前，我需要确认——店长对餐馆的后续发展有何规划？
t_en: But before we open for the day, I need to confirm. What's your vision for the restaurant's future?

t_ch: 身为主管，我需要依照你的想法制定经营方案。
t_en: As the supervisor, I'll need to plan accordingly based on your direction.

t_ch: 确实需要一个长期的经营目标呢。那就麻烦{NICKNAME}为大家选一个努力的方向？
t_en: A long-term goal really would help. {NICKNAME}, mind picking a direction for us to aim toward?

t_ch: 做大、做强？
t_en: Be bigger and better?

t_ch: 作为目标而言，似乎有些空泛了，需要更明确的判断标准。
t_en: A little vague for a goal. We need something that can be evaluated more concretely.

t_ch: 赚更多钱？
t_en: Make more money?

t_ch: *我们*记得，村长提出的委托是振兴餐馆，营业只是顺带的啦。
t_en: We remember the village chief's request was to revitalize the restaurant. Making a profit was more of a side effect.

t_ch: 成为米其林三星级餐馆。
t_en: Become a Memchelin 3-star restaurant.

t_ch: 这个想法不错！大家聚在一起，克服万难提升评级，最后成为最受欢迎的餐馆，不觉得是个很有趣的故事吗？
t_en: Now that sounds fun! Everyone working together, overcoming challenges to raise our rating, and becoming the most beloved restaurant in the end! That's a fun story, don't you think?

t_ch: 正巧，我在过来路上捡到了这个。
t_en: As it happens, I picked this up on the way here.

t_ch: 我们的小店还没被收录进名单里，没有星级呢。
t_en: Our little place isn't even listed yet. So, no stars to speak of.

t_ch: 既然评审会定期举行，不如…<color=#dbc291ff>就以成为迷其林四星餐馆，作为最终目标吧！</color>
t_en: Since the review is held regularly... why don't we <color=#dbc291ff>set our ultimate goal as becoming a Memchelin four-star restaurant?</color>

t_ch: 就这么办吧。
t_en: Sounds like a plan.

t_ch: 还比三星多一星？
t_en: One more star than three?

t_ch: 会不会有点太巧了…
t_en: That's... suspiciously convenient.

t_ch: 这是你现编的设定吧…
t_en: You totally just made that up on the spot, didn't you?

t_ch: 「哎呀，只是借用下你提到的名字，我保证，后续发展绝对是原创情节哦。」
t_en: "Oh, I only borrowed the name you mentioned. I promise the rest of the story will be completely original!"

t_ch: 我倒是无妨。这是个打响名声的好机会，若是能提升评级，也会引来更多客流。
t_en: I don't mind. It's a golden opportunity to build our reputation. If we can boost our rating, we'll attract even more customers.

t_ch: 只是把菜品送去评审的话，也不会影响正常营业。
t_en: Besides, simply submitting our dishes for review won't interfere with regular operations.

t_ch: 那就决定啦。{NICKNAME}，你说不定会成为迷境里第一个四星餐馆的主理人哦？
t_en: Then it's settled! {NICKNAME}, you just might become the very first four-star restaurant owner in the entire Maze!

t_ch: 目标——四星餐馆！
t_en: Goal — Four-Star Restaurant!

t_ch: 好有热情呀，我也来！目标——四星！
t_en: Such energy. Count me in! Goal — Four stars!

t_ch: 听起来还不赖。
t_en: Sounds good to me.

t_ch: 就是很厉害呀。四星级代表着妖精们的最高认可，是莫大的荣誉哦！
t_en: It's really impressive, actually. A four-star rating means the highest possible praise from the fairies. It's a huge honor!

t_ch: 小灰，还有件事别忘啦。指南里有说，参加评审需要填写餐馆的名字。
t_en: Little Gray, don't forget there's something else. The guidebook says we need to submit the restaurant's name when we enter the review.

t_ch: 还真是，大家一直都在用「妖精餐馆」这个称呼。
t_en: Right! We've all just been calling it "The Fairy Restaurant" this whole time.

t_ch: 命名这种事，就交给我们的店长决定了。得足够威风，足够有吸引力。
t_en: Well, let's leave the naming to our manager. It's gotta sound impressive, and appealing too.

t_ch: 取名字…这个难题曾多次让你焦头烂额。你重新搜刮起脑海中的知识储备，试图从其中挑选出最符合心意的答案。
t_en: Ah, naming... a task that has driven you to the brink many times before. Once again, you search through your mental archives, hoping to find the perfect name that captures your vision.

t_ch: 我经营的餐馆，就叫做——
t_en: My restaurant shall be called...

t_ch: 开拓者之家。
t_en: The Trailblazer's Hearth.

t_ch: 呵。用自身为城邦命名，颇有君主的气概。不错，我很中意。
t_en: Heh. Naming your domain after yourself... Now that's the audacity of a true monarch. Excellent, I approve.

t_ch: 如何？若是决定了，我就记入档案里。
t_en: Well? If that's your final choice, I'll make it official.

t_ch: 就用这个。
t_en: Let's go with that.

t_ch: 还是再想想吧…
t_en: Best to think it over...

t_ch: 迷境大饭店。
t_en: Maze Grand Restaurant.

t_ch: *我们*喜欢这个名字，会联想到好多好吃的，听起来就很气派。
t_en: We love that one! Makes us think of a banquet full of tasty treats. Sounds super grand, too!

t_ch: 如何？若是决定了，我就记入档案里。
t_en: Well? If that's your final choice, I'll make it official.

t_ch: 就用这个。
t_en: Let's go with that.

t_ch: 还是再想想吧…
t_en: Best to think it over...

t_ch: 哀丽秘榭社区食堂。
t_en: Aedes Elysiae Community Canteen.

t_ch: 我对这个名字倒是没什么意见，只可惜，村子里的大家没办法来迷境里用餐啊。
t_en: I've got no issue with that name. Just a shame no one from the village can actually come dine in the Maze.

t_ch: 如何？若是决定了，我就记入档案里。
t_en: Well? If that's your final choice, I'll make it official.

t_ch: 就用这个。
t_en: Let's go with that.

t_ch: 还是再想想吧…
t_en: Best to think it over...

t_ch: 黄金裔活动中心。
t_en: Chrysos Heir Activity Center.

t_ch: 啊，又在说奇怪的话了。但既然是你喜欢的名字，肯定有什么特别的用意吧。
t_en: Ah, saying weird things again. But if you like it, I'm sure there's some special meaning behind it.

t_ch: 如何？若是决定了，我就记入档案里。
t_en: Well? If that's your final choice, I'll make it official.

t_ch: 就用这个。
t_en: Let's go with that.

t_ch: 还是再想想吧…
t_en: Best to think it over...

t_ch: 自定义名称2。
t_en: Custom Name 2.

t_ch: …我的爱臣都以缄默为美德，看来这次，我得向他们学习才是。
t_en: ...My dear subjects all consider silence a virtue. Seems I should learn from them this time.

t_ch: 如何？若是决定了，我就记入档案里。
t_en: Well? If that's your final choice, I'll make it official.

t_ch: 就用这个。
t_en: Let's go with that.

t_ch: 还是再想想吧…
t_en: Best to think it over...

t_ch: 记好了，餐馆的新名号就定为「{TEXTJOIN#225}」。
t_en: Got it. From this moment on, the restaurant shall be known as "{TEXTJOIN#225}."

t_ch: 美食评审会将在今日营业结束后召开，此前，各位就先以提升餐馆营业额为目标吧。
t_en: The gourmet review will be held after closing today. Until then, everyone, focus on boosting our revenue.

t_ch: 若是连基本的营收目标都达不成，去参加评审也不过是引人发笑罢了。
t_en: If we can't even meet our baseline income target, showing up for the review would just make us a laughingstock.

t_ch: 等等，还得再加上一条。咱们开店的目的是为妖精提供美食，必须以菜品为核心。
t_en: Oh, and one more thing. Our mission is to serve the fairies great food. That means our dishes must remain front and center.

t_ch: …再争论下去，妖精们的肚皮可都要饿瘪啦。剩下的问题，等餐馆开门后再说吧。
t_en: If we keep arguing, the fairies' stomachs will start growling. Let's save the rest for after we open the doors.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，拜托你了——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we'll be counting on you!

t_ch: 我现在宣布……
t_en: Now, I hereby declare...

t_ch: 「{TEXTJOIN#225}」，开始营业！
t_en: "{TEXTJOIN#225}" is officially open for business!

t_ch: 方案？不是很懂…只要吸引更多小妖精来用餐就好了吧？
t_en: A plan? I don't really get that stuff... Isn't it enough if more fairies come to eat?

t_ch: 单论增加客流量，便有低价促销与提升翻台率两种策略可选，对团队的要求也各不相同。顾客增加也会带来更高的管理成本，未必一定是好事。
t_en: If we're just talking traffic, we've got two approaches: run discounts, or boost table turnover. Each one puts different demands on the team. And more customers means more management pressure, which is not always a good thing.

t_ch: 迷！肚皮鼓鼓的，心里暖呼呼的，好高兴迷！
t_en: Mem! My tummy's full and my heart's all warm. I'm so happy, mem!

t_ch: 鼓掌！今天的营业也顺利结束啦。
t_en: Applause! Today's business wrapped up without a hitch.

t_ch: 呼…没想到顾客增加后，厨房工作这么上强度，但特别有成就感啊。
t_en: Whew... I wasn't expecting the kitchen work to ramp up that much with more customers. But I must say, it felt really rewarding.

t_ch: 小灰的表现也很棒，要夸夸！
t_en: Little Gray did great too. Worthy of praise!

t_ch: 是不是有人没来？
t_en: Wait, isn't someone missing?

t_ch: 对哦。似乎没见到阿格莱雅？
t_en: Oh right. I don't think we saw Aglaea?

t_ch: 若是意指金织爵，我有件要事交代她去处理，想来明日便能见到结果。
t_en: If you mean the Dux Goldweaver, I sent her on an important errand. We should see the results tomorrow.

t_ch: 不记作旷工，就当她是「因公出差」吧。
t_en: No need to mark her absent. Let's call it an "official business trip."

t_ch: 能不能多夸点？
t_en: Can I get more compliments?

t_ch: 迷！谢谢你们，谢谢店长！我们还会来光顾的迷！
t_en: Mem! Thank you all, and thank you, manager! We'll definitely be coming back, mem!

t_ch: 妖精们对你的感谢，都写在鼓鼓胀胀的小肚子上啦。
t_en: The fairies' gratitude is written all over their full, happy bellies.

t_ch: 离不开各位的努力。
t_en: None of this would've been possible without everyone's hard work.

t_ch: 餐馆的事处理得差不多，该去参加那个迷其林美食评审了。
t_en: Now that everything's settled at the restaurant, it's time for the Memchelin Food Review.

t_ch: 我还很好奇，自己的菜品会得到什么样的评价呢。
t_en: I'm actually curious... What kind of feedback will my dishes get?

t_ch: 行呀。准备好了就出发吧。
t_en: Alright. Let's head out when you're ready.

t_ch: 美食评审会就要开始啦，做好出发的准备了吗？
t_en: The food review is about to begin. Ready to hit the road?

t_ch: 走走走，出发。
t_en: Come on, let's get going!

t_ch: 第一次参加评审会，有些紧张呢。不知道会收获什么样的评价？
t_en: It's our first time at a review like this. I'm somewhat nervous... Wonder what kind of score we'll get?

t_ch: 希望是个好的开始。走吧。
t_en: Here's hoping it's a great start. Come on, let's move.

t_ch: 还有点事要处理…
t_en: Still got a couple of things to wrap up...

t_ch: 没关系。准备好了，记得来找我们哦。
t_en: No worries. Once you're ready, just come find us.

t_ch: 迷迷迷…迷迷！迷其林美食评审大会即将开始，请做好准备迷！
t_en: Memem... Mem! The Memchelin Food Review is about to begin. Please get ready, mem!

t_ch: 小妖精们好认真呀，原来是这么严肃的场合吗？
t_en: The fairies are really serious about this. Is it that formal of an event?

t_ch: 妖精对待美食的态度非常严格，都说食品质量无小事嘛。
t_en: Fairies hold food to the highest standards. As we all know, there's no matter too trivial in food quality.

t_ch: 本着公平、公正、公开的原则，评审员将现场品尝菜品，并给出最终的判断迷！
t_en: In the spirit of fairness, impartiality, and transparency, our judges will taste each dish on-site and deliver their final verdict, mem!

t_ch: 现在，迷！请将送审菜品摆到指定空桌上迷！
t_en: Now then, mem! Please place your submitted dish on the designated table, mem!

t_ch: 需要选出代表性的菜品呢。我们的招牌菜，就交给店长决定啦。
t_en: We'll need something that best represents our restaurant. The choice of our signature dish now rests in your hands.

t_ch: 美食评审，这可是关系到餐馆评级的大事，当然得仔细思考后再做决定。你在脑海里想象着评审员品尝这道菜品的画面，心中总算有了答案。
t_en: This is a huge moment. The food review can make or break a restaurant's reputation. This calls for a thorough deliberation. You picture the judges savoring the dish... And finally, the answer comes to your mind.

t_ch: 我们餐馆准备的菜品是——
t_en: The dish we're presenting is—

t_ch: 缤纷沙拉。
t_en: Colorful Salad.

t_ch: 生菜沙拉。
t_en: Lettuce Salad.

t_ch: 明白了。我来把店长指定的菜品摆放在桌上。
t_en: Got it. I'll place the dish you chose onto the table.

t_ch: 除传统的两位妖精评审员外，本次评选会还邀请到了一位神秘嘉宾！
t_en: Aside from the two traditional fairy judges, we've also invited a mystery guest for this review!

t_ch: 据说，这位特邀评审员曾在各地漫游，品鉴过的美食不计其数，尤其擅长烹制生鲜食材，更是享有「厨房杀手」的美誉！
t_en: Word has it she's traveled far and wide, tasted countless delicacies, and is especially skilled at preparing raw ingredients. They say she's earned the title... "The Kitchen Slayer!"

t_ch: 她的加入，会为「迷其林美食评审」带来怎样的变数呢？敬请期待迷！
t_en: What kind of twist will this special guest bring to the Memchelin Food Review? Stay tuned and find out, mem!

t_ch: 你该不会在紧张吧？
t_en: You're not getting nervous, are you?

t_ch: 哈、哈哈，当然不会，我已经是个成熟的厨师了，这种评审当然不在话下。没问题的！
t_en: Ha, haha... Of course not! I'm a professional chef now. A little food review's nothing! Totally under control!

t_ch: 这位神秘的特邀嘉宾究竟会是谁呢，先前没听村长说过呀。
t_en: Who could this mysterious special guest be? The village chief never mentioned anything about this beforehand.

t_ch: 我猜也是熟人。
t_en: I've got a feeling it's a familiar face.

t_ch: 要不做个排除法吧？
t_en: Shall we use the method of elimination?

t_ch: 啊！*我们*看到了！那位新来的评审员就是——
t_en: Ah! We see her! That new judge is—

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，久疏问候。没想到…会在此处与各位相见。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, it's been a while. I didn't expect to meet you all here.

t_ch: 遐蝶小姐？原来妖精们请来的评审员是你啊。
t_en: Miss Castorice? So, you're the special guest judge the fairies invited.

t_ch: 阿蝶！你也是来找小妖精们玩的吗？
t_en: Cas! You here to play with the little fairies too?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，我原本应聘了餐馆的主厨职位，却因为手艺不精，没能通过村长的考核。
t_en: I'm a bit embarrassed to say it, but I actually applied to be the restaurant's chef. Unfortunately, my skills didn't pass the village chief's assessment.

t_ch: 好在，我对美食品鉴还有些心得，便接受了妖精们的邀请，来担任特邀评审员的职责。
t_en: Still, I do have a decent palate when it comes to gourmet dishes. So I accepted the fairies' invitation to act as a guest judge today.

t_ch: 都是熟人，让我们直接通过吧。
t_en: We're all friends here, maybe just let us pass?

t_ch: 这个…恐怕不太行。评审结果由我与另外两位代表投票得出，我没法独自决定。
t_en: That... I'm afraid I can't. The results are determined by a vote between me and the two fairy judges. I can't decide it alone.

t_ch: 你有一票肯定权吗？
t_en: Do you have the deciding vote?

t_ch: 没有…为秉持公平原则，评审结果将由我与两位妖精代表投票得出。
t_en: No... To keep things fair, the results will be jointly decided by me and two fairy judges.

t_ch: 既然菜品已经布置妥当，我就不客气了。
t_en: Now that the dishes are in place, I'll help myself.

t_ch: （好像除了这两道沙拉，餐馆也没别的菜可选了……）
t_en: (Well, these two salads are the only menu options here...)

t_ch: 唔。
t_en: Hmm.

t_ch: 迷！迷迷！迷迷迷！
t_en: Mem! Memem, mememem!

t_ch: 嗯，我也同意。那么，就由我来宣布本次美食评审大会的结果——
t_en: I agree as well. Then, allow me to announce the outcome of this Food Review—

t_ch: 各位准备的菜品，相当优秀。
t_en: Your dishes were excellent.

t_ch: 这就要问我们的主厨了。
t_en: That would be a question for our head chef.

t_ch: 因为能使用的食材只有农田种植的蔬菜，并且，我认为沙拉是种极为出色的菜品。
t_en: Because we only have farm-grown vegetables to work with. Also, I genuinely think salad is a fantastic dish.

t_ch: 因为农田里种不出肉。
t_en: Because we can't grow meat in the fields.

t_ch: 这是其中一个原因。另外，我认为沙拉是种极为出色的菜品。
t_en: That's part of it. But also, I truly believe salads are a wonderfully crafted culinary form.

t_ch: …原来如此。希望以后还能有机会，继续品尝白厄阁下的菜品。
t_en: ...Understood. I look forward to the chance to taste more of your dishes, Lord Phainon.

t_ch: 作为通过本次美食评审的证明，我将为店长颁发迷其林星级奖章。
t_en: As proof that you've passed this Food Review, I hereby award the Memchelin Star Award to you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，请收下这个。
t_en: Please accept this, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 迷！我们郑重宣布——<color=#dbc291ff>「{TEXTJOIN#225}」正式收录进迷其林美食名单中，是一星级餐馆了迷！</color>
t_en: Mem! We hereby announce — <color=#dbc291ff>"{TEXTJOIN#225}" is now officially listed in the Memchelin Gourmet Guide as a 1-star Restaurant, mem!</color>

t_ch: 要继续努力，朝着四星级的目标前进迷！迷！
t_en: Keep it up! Aim for that four-star goal next! Mem!

t_ch: 真正的米其林不是升星制吧？
t_en: The real Michelin system doesn't increase stars like this...

t_ch: 别在意。要是一口气就达到目标的话，多没意思呀。
t_en: Don't sweat the details. If you reach the top right away, it'd ruin all the fun, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 无论如何，餐馆的评级提升，后续也能吸引来更多顾客，确实值得庆祝。
t_en: Either way, a higher restaurant rating means more customers will be coming your way. Definitely something to celebrate.

t_ch: 好耶！成为星级餐馆了！
t_en: Woohoo! We're a starred restaurant now!

t_ch: 人家也来——星级餐馆！轻轻松松！
t_en: Hooray! A starred restaurant! Easy-peasy!

t_ch: 很好。餐馆的评级提升，后续也能吸引更多顾客来就餐，确实值得庆祝。
t_en: Indeed. With the upgraded rating, more customers are sure to stop by for a meal. It's something worth celebrating.

t_ch: 要忙碌起来了呢。不过是好事！
t_en: Looks like we're about to get a lot busier! But that's a good thing.

t_ch: 亲手制作的菜品得到了肯定，我现在心中充满了斗志。
t_en: Having my cooking officially recognized... I feel fired up all over now!

t_ch: 美食评审顺利通过，大家都辛苦啦。发生了好多有趣的事呢，现在回去休息吧。
t_en: We passed the Food Review. Great work, everyone. It's been quite the day. Let's head back now and take a break.

t_ch: 太好了！
t_en: Yes!

t_ch: 入口便能感受到蔬菜清爽的口感，浇淋其上的酱汁更是恰到好处地丰富了味道层次。
t_en: The freshness of the vegetables was immediately apparent, and the dressing added just the right depth of flavor.

t_ch: 刚开业的餐馆，能提供如此美味的菜品实属难得。可以看出，{NICKNAME}阁下的管理无疑很出色。
t_en: For a newly opened restaurant to offer cuisine of this quality is rare. Clearly, your management has been outstanding, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 但…我有个疑问。为何菜单上全都是沙拉呢？
t_en: However... I do have one question. Why is the menu entirely made up of salads?

t_ch: 呼，有点累了。虽然通过了评审很开心，但餐馆还要继续营业，可别忘了哦。
t_en: Phew, I'm exhausted but thrilled we passed. Just remember, the restaurant still needs to open tomorrow.

t_ch: 时间不早啦，{NICKNAME}也回去休息吧。
t_en: It's getting late. {NICKNAME}, you should rest too.

t_ch: 明天别忘了来上班。
t_en: Don't forget to come to work tomorrow.

t_ch: 呀，真严格呢。人家当然会记得~明天见。
t_en: Aww, so strict~ Don't worry, I'll be here bright and early tomorrow. See ya~

t_ch: 我还不想休息。
t_en: I'm not ready to rest yet.

t_ch: 对哦，{NICKNAME}还有很多事情要忙吧，那就快快处理好，我会在这里等你。
t_en: Oh, that's right. {NICKNAME}, you've still got a lot to do, huh? Then hop to it. I'll be waiting right here.

t_ch: …感觉不太对啊。
t_en: ...Something feels off.

t_ch: 小白，*我们*来帮你看着！
t_en: Snowy, we'll help you keep an eye out!

t_ch: 唔，要向左挪一点。对，再向左！
t_en: Um... a bit more to the left. Yes, more to the left!

t_ch: 现在呢？
t_en: How about now?

t_ch: 呵。依我看，当前位置明显是偏左了。
t_en: Heh. If you ask me, it's clearly too far left now.

t_ch: 图案中轴线与门框对不上，边缘也超出不少。该朝右侧移动才是。
t_en: The center of the design doesn't line up with the door frame, and the edges are sticking out. You need to move it to the right.

t_ch: 不对！小凯撒虽然脾气大，眼神却不太行。应该向左！
t_en: Wrong! Little Imperator's got a big temper but terrible eyesight. It should go left!

t_ch: 命运爵，是你的视角太低了。若是站在高位，便能清楚看到朝右才是正解。
t_en: Dux Fatorum, your angle's too low. From a higher vantage point, it's obvious — it needs to go right.

t_ch: 小凯撒，欺负人！*我们*以前可比你高多了！
t_en: Little Imperator's being mean! We used to be much taller than you!

t_ch: 哎，我还是自己判断吧。
t_en: Well, guess I'll just judge for myself.

t_ch: 不知为何，今日的餐馆门口格外喧嚣。等你赶到时，才发现……
t_en: For some reason, the front of the restaurant is unusually noisy today. By the time you arrive...

t_ch: 小灰！快来看小白的劳动成果！
t_en: Little Gray, come check out Snowy's great work!

t_ch: 怎么样。哼哼，是不是很有大饭店的感觉啦？
t_en: Pretty impressive, huh? Feels like a fancy restaurant now, doesn't it?

t_ch: 餐馆正式挂牌了！
t_en: The sign is up! We're official!

t_ch: 这就是星级餐馆的模样吗？
t_en: So this is what a starred restaurant looks like?

t_ch: 餐馆获得了迷其林星级，店面当然得跟着升级才行。
t_en: Now that we've earned a Memchelin Star, the storefront needs to keep up with our new image.

t_ch: 所以我拜托妖精们赶制了新的桌椅和招牌。别看就这么小小一块，装起来还挺累的。
t_en: That's why I asked the fairies to rush out new tables, chairs, and a fresh sign. It may look small, but putting it up was surprisingly tiring.

t_ch: 辛苦啦。小凯撒会给你记加班！
t_en: You've done great. Little Imperator will mark you down for overtime!

t_ch: 既然店长到了，我们便开始讨论后续的经营事宜吧。
t_en: Now that the manager's here, let's discuss our future plans.

t_ch: 获得迷其林星级后，客流量也将增加，餐馆的各项标准自然需要随之提高。
t_en: With our new star rating, more customers will be showing up. Naturally, the restaurant's standards must rise in every aspect to meet demand.

t_ch: 首先，正如那位评审员所言——目前的菜单实在过分单调了。
t_en: First of all, just like that judge said, the current menu is way too limited.

t_ch: 想扩充菜单的话，奶油生菜沙拉怎么样？
t_en: If we're expanding the menu, how about a Creamy Lettuce Salad?

t_ch: 拒绝！不要再新增任何沙拉啦。
t_en: No! Absolutely no more salads.

t_ch: 虽然糖分摄入有所增加，但口感会更丰富，清爽的蔬果可以开胃，也适合作为餐后甜点。
t_en: Sure, the sugar content's a bit higher, but it's more flavorful. Refreshing fruit and veggies can whet the appetite, and they also work great as a dessert.

t_ch: 小白，别太爱沙拉了。大家还有其他想法吗？
t_en: Snowy, stop being so obsessed with salads. Anyone else got ideas?

t_ch: 说起来，先前还有位厨师应聘了主厨职位。
t_en: Speaking of which, wasn't there another person that applied for the chef position earlier?

t_ch: 只是他住的地方很远，过来哀丽秘榭得多花些时间。算算…今天该到了吧？
t_en: Yeah, but he lives pretty far from here. Coming all the way to Aedes Elysiae takes time. If I'm not wrong... he should be arriving today.

t_ch: 听说贵餐馆有位大厨在，我还以为是什么厉害角色。
t_en: I heard your restaurant had a chef. Honestly, I was expecting them to be someone more impressive.

t_ch: 是万敌，餐馆有救了。
t_en: It's Mydei! The restaurant is saved!

t_ch: 还有谁没登场来着？
t_en: Now, who's still missing from the cast?

t_ch: 迈德漠斯？没想到新厨师是你。
t_en: Mydeimos? Never thought you'd be the new chef.

t_ch: 正好，上次竞赛你我胜负未分。不如借此机会，让我们在厨房再比试一场。
t_en: Perfect timing. We never settled the score from that last contest. How about we settle it in this kitchen?

t_ch: 随时奉陪。不过得先让我熟悉这里。
t_en: Anytime. But I'll need to get familiar with the place first.

t_ch: 带我去看看吧。
t_en: Come, show me around.

t_ch: 好呀。*我们*来给小敌引路！
t_en: Ooh, great! We will lead the way for De!

t_ch: 我算是明白，这家餐馆的菜单为何如此简陋了。
t_en: Now I see why this restaurant's menu is so minimalist.

t_ch: 连口像样的锅都没有，还怎么做菜？难怪卖的都是草。
t_en: Not even a decent pot in sight. How do you expect to cook properly? No wonder the menu's just grass and leaves.

t_ch: 那就请我们的新主厨说说吧，你有什么主意？
t_en: Then let's hear it from our new chef here — what do you have in mind?

t_ch: 哼，看好了。
t_en: Hmph. Watch closely.

t_ch: 万敌带着几个妖精忙碌了起来。不多时，厨房门口便架起了一口大锅。
t_en: Mydei and the fairies spring into action. Before long, a massive pot is set up right outside the kitchen door.

t_ch: 早听说悬锋城的基建速度极为惊人，还真是…百闻不如一见啊。
t_en: I've heard the construction in Castrum Kremnos is ridiculously fast, but seeing it in action... really puts things into perspective.

t_ch: 专业的厨师，没几样趁手的厨具怎么行。
t_en: A pro chef without proper tools just won't cut it.

t_ch: 听你们说，想扩充菜单？让我看看都有什么食材。
t_en: So, you want to expand the menu? Let me check what ingredients you've got.

t_ch: 刚才小风堇送来了农田收获的蔬菜，小敌可以试试！
t_en: Cinny just dropped off some veggies from the farm. You can try using those, De!

t_ch: 好。这就开火。
t_en: Perfect. Let's start the fire.

t_ch: 我整理了昨日的经营数据和客诉，发现餐馆还有些亟待解决的问题。
t_en: I reviewed yesterday's business data and customer feedback. There are a few issues we need to address.

t_ch: 其一，随着客流量提升，服务员的数量明显不够，顾客等候上菜的时间太过漫长。
t_en: First, with the increase in customers, we clearly don't have enough servers. The wait time for food has gotten way too long.

t_ch: 这个有办法！还有很多小妖精想来餐馆工作，*我们*再去找点就好。
t_en: That's easy! Tons of little fairies want to work here. We can always recruit extra hands.

t_ch: 其二，用餐区域的桌椅同样需要扩充，但目前，这个职能还没有对应的负责人。
t_en: Second, we need more tables and chairs for the dining area, but there's no one in charge of that role right now.

t_ch: 确实呢。不如，现在就让店长为餐馆指定一位「大堂经理」吧。
t_en: That's true. Why not appoint a "lobby manager" right now, manager?

t_ch: 要不让昔涟来担任？
t_en: Would you like to take the job, Cyrene?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}这么信任我，是很高兴啦。但人家还有许多事要处理，招聘、团队管理、营业报告、陪小妖精们聊天、宣布打烊……
t_en: Aww, {NICKNAME}, I'm flattered you trust me! But I've already got so much on my plate — recruiting, team management, business reports, chatting with the fairies, calling closing time...

t_ch: 不行不行，怕忙不过来，给大家添麻烦。
t_en: Nuh-uh. I'd just slow everyone down.

t_ch: 白厄是个不错的人选。
t_en: Phainon might be a good fit.

t_ch: 嗯，应该没问题。万敌会分担部分厨房工作，我可以空出来支援大堂。
t_en: I think I can manage. With Mydei sharing some of the kitchen duties, I'll have more time to help out front.

t_ch: 正好，我和妖精们很熟悉，找些帮手来制作桌椅也方便。
t_en: Plus, I know the fairies well. It'll be easy to get help making more furniture.

t_ch: 缇宝介意打两份工吗？
t_en: Tribbie, you mind picking up a second job?

t_ch: 虽说*我们*有三个人，但搬桌子这种工作还是太困难啦。小灰问问其余伙伴吧？
t_en: Even with three of us, moving tables is little tough. Maybe you can check with the rest of the crew?

t_ch: 我亲自上也不是不行…
t_en: I could do it myself...

t_ch: 热心工作是好事，但{NICKNAME}还有许多事要忙吧？就别兼职啦。
t_en: Love the enthusiasm, but {NICKNAME}, you've got a lot on your plate too. Better leave the heavy lifting to us.

t_ch: 那就由白厄来担任大堂经理。
t_en: Then it's settled. Phainon will take over as lobby manager.

t_ch: 明白。我来记进档案，后续工作就麻烦两位了。没有其他事的话，就散会吧。
t_en: Got it. I'll update the records, and the follow-up will be in your capable hands. If there's nothing else, this meeting is adjourned.

t_ch: 至于实际解决方案，就交由店长决定了。
t_en: As for how to tackle the actual issues, that's for you to decide, manager.

t_ch: 这是我的职责。
t_en: Well, it's part of my job.

t_ch: …不会又要学好久吧？
t_en: ...This isn't going to take forever to learn again, is it?

t_ch: 放心啦，*我们*和小白会尽可能简单处理的，保证小灰一眼就能看懂！
t_en: No worries! We and Snowy will keep things super simple. You'll understand everything at a glance!

t_ch: 各位！开始营业前，有件事想和大家商量。
t_en: Everyone! Before we open for the day, there's something I'd like to discuss.

t_ch: 咱们的餐馆已经小有名气啦，日常经营也走上了正轨，是时候尝试些更有意思的内容了。
t_en: Our restaurant's starting to make a name for itself, and day-to-day operations are running smoothly. I think it's time we try something more fun.

t_ch: *我们*同意！但是，有意思的内容，会是什么呢？
t_en: We're in! But what do you mean by "fun"?

t_ch: 城邦里的店铺不是经常会庆祝节日嘛，譬如法吉娜的狂欢月，还有纪念云石天宫的建成。
t_en: You know how shops in the city-state host seasonal celebrations? Like Phagousa's Month of Carnival, or the anniversary of the Marmoreal Palace?

t_ch: 我想，餐馆是不是也可以仿照类似的做法，定期推出有吸引力的…嗯，新菜单？或者装扮下店内环境也好，总之就是要有变化。
t_en: Perhaps our restaurant could adopt a similar approach, regularly... refreshing the menu with enticing new dishes? Or even revamping the decor? The key is to keep things fresh and exciting.

t_ch: 我知道，是主题活动。
t_en: Ah! You're talking about themed events.

t_ch: 对对！主题活动，果然还是{NICKNAME}见多识广呀。
t_en: Yes! Themed events! I knew {NICKNAME} would get it!

t_ch: 大家可以提出自己心仪的主题，至于决定…就采用投票制怎么样？
t_en: Everyone can pitch their own ideas for themes, and we'll decide by vote. How does that sound?

t_ch: 最后由{NICKNAME}统一通过，提议者来担任活动负责人。主题定期轮换，不会很辛苦的。
t_en: {NICKNAME} will have final say. Whoever suggests the chosen theme will take the lead for that event. We'll rotate themes regularly, so it won't be too much work.

t_ch: 开放式选题哦。欢迎踊跃发言。
t_en: The floor's open! Feel free to speak up~

t_ch: 话虽如此，我一时半会也想不到什么有意思的。
t_en: That said... I'm kind of drawing a blank.

t_ch: …确实好难哦。
t_en: ...It is hard indeed.

t_ch: 呵。既然各位不愿献上谏言，那便由我来主导吧。
t_en: Heh. Since no one's willing to step up, allow me to take the reins.

t_ch: 我——奥赫玛同盟的领袖，圣城的凯撒——在此倡议，为表敬意与纪念，餐馆的首个经营活动。
t_en: In honor and commemoration, I — leader of the Okhema Alliance, Imperator of the holy city — hereby propose the first-ever restaurant event.

t_ch: <size=28>应当以「凯撒」作为主题！</size>
t_en: <size=28>The event theme shall be: "Imperator"!</size>

t_ch: 活动期间内，所有菜品必须以「凯撒」的名号作为前缀，所有员工必须以臣子自称，所有顾客在进餐前必先称颂「凯撒」的伟绩。
t_en: During the event, all dishes must be prefixed with the noble title "Imperator," all staff shall refer to themselves as loyal subjects, and all customers must recite praises of the Imperator's glory before every meal.

t_ch: 而先前的沙拉菜品，按规范，此时便应更名为——
t_en: Thus, that earlier salad dish shall henceforth be renamed—

t_ch: <size=28>「凯撒沙拉」。</size>
t_en: <size=28>"Cery Salad."</size>

t_ch: …有点冷了。
t_en: ...That's a bit of a dry joke.

t_ch: 你也令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: You do have a knack for cracking people up.

t_ch: 我，哀丽秘榭的白厄，实名反对这个提案。
t_en: I, Phainon of Aedes Elysiae, reject this proposal with full conviction.

t_ch: *我们*也反对！
t_en: We reject it too!

t_ch: 呼…太好了，人家还在担心这个主题会真的通过呢。
t_en: Whew... I was really worried for a second that theme might actually pass.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈！这何尝不是一种破局的方法呢？
t_en: Hahahahaha! Well, at least that's one way to shake things up.

t_ch: 既然思想的火苗已被点燃，我就不打扰各位继续沉思了。
t_en: Now that the spark of creativity has been lit, I'll leave you to your brainstorming.

t_ch: 我有个想法。既然是迷境里的餐馆，不如就以「哀丽秘榭」为主题怎么样？
t_en: I have a proposal. Since we're a restaurant in the Maze, how about a theme inspired by Aedes Elysiae itself?

t_ch: 提起哀丽秘榭，首先想到的便是麦田。活动期间，餐馆提供以小麦为原料的菜品，并用饱满的麦穗作为装饰，寓意丰收。
t_en: When I think of Aedes Elysiae, the golden wheat fields come to mind. During the event, we could serve wheat-based dishes and decorate the place with bundles of wheat, symbolizing a bountiful harvest.

t_ch: 但「哀丽秘榭」作为主题，对小妖精们而言会不会太难懂了呀。
t_en: But if the theme is "Aedes Elysiae," wouldn't that be a bit too difficult for the little fairies to understand?

t_ch: *我们*想到了！第一次办活动，当然要有吸引力才行！
t_en: We have an idea! For the first event, it has to be something super appealing!

t_ch: 就拿所有人都喜欢的菜作为主题吧！猜猜看，会是什么呢？
t_en: Let's build the theme around the dish everyone loves! Can you guess?

t_ch: 答案是甜食！*我们*知道，没有人能抵抗住甜品的诱惑。
t_en: The answer is sweets! We know no one can resist the charm of desserts.

t_ch: 活动主题就是——「甜蜜蜜节」！
t_en: The event theme will be — "Sweetie Festival!"

t_ch: 好呀。两位都提出了自己的方案，现在开始投票！每人只有一票哦，得想清楚啦。
t_en: Alright! Both proposals are on the table. Time to vote! One vote per person, so think carefully.

t_ch: 支持白厄主厨的，请举手！
t_en: Anyone in favor of Chef Phainon's proposal, raise your hand!

t_ch: （举手）
t_en: (Raises hand)

t_ch: 白厄还有{NICKNAME}，目前共计两票呢。
t_en: That makes two votes for Phainon — his and {NICKNAME}'s.

t_ch: （不举手）
t_en: (Don't raise your hand)

t_ch: 很遗憾，只有白厄投给了自己呢。
t_en: Oh dear, only Phainon voted for himself.

t_ch: 想要支持缇宝老师的，请举手！
t_en: Now, those in favor of Lady Tribbie's proposal, raise your hand!

t_ch: *我们*举手！
t_en: We vote for it!

t_ch: （举手）
t_en: (Raises hand)

t_ch: 店长…都说每人只有一票，不许滥用职权哦。
t_en: Manager... we said one vote per person. No abusing your power.

t_ch: 缇宝、{NICKNAME}，再加上我…共计是三票。
t_en: So that's Tribbie, {NICKNAME}, and me... three votes in total.

t_ch: （不举手）
t_en: (Don't raise your hand)

t_ch: 缇宝再加上我，总共是两票。
t_en: Alright, guess it's just Tribbie and me — two votes in total.

t_ch: 好吧，弃权也是一种选择。缇宝再加上我，总共是两票。
t_en: Fair enough. Abstaining counts too. So that's two votes for Tribbie's proposal.

t_ch: 既然如此，最终结果是……
t_en: Which means the final tally is—

t_ch: 算错啦。{RUBY_B#缇宝、缇安和缇宁}*我们*{RUBY_E#}都举手了，该算作三票！压倒性的优势呢！
t_en: Uh-uh, you miscounted. {RUBY_B#Tribbie, Trianne and Trinnon}We{RUBY_E#} all raised our hands! That's three votes! A landslide victory!
Huh!?

t_ch: …我认输。没有人能在投票表决里胜过缇宝老师。
t_en: ...I yield. No one can win over Tribbie when it comes to voting.

t_ch: 那就决定啦！餐馆的第一个主题活动是「甜蜜蜜节」，负责人则是缇宝、缇安与缇宁。
t_en: Then it's settled! The restaurant's first themed event will be "Sweetie Festival," and the event leads will be Tribbie, Trianne, and Trinnon.

t_ch: 店长同意这个安排嘛？
t_en: Manager, do you approve of this arrangement?

t_ch: 我没意见。
t_en: Fine by me.

t_ch: 办得不好要负责任的哦？
t_en: You'll be held responsible if it doesn't go well, you know?

t_ch: 放心。*我们*不会让小灰失望的！
t_en: Don't worry. We won't let you down!

t_ch: …剪刀、石头、布！
t_en: ...Rock, paper, scissors!

t_ch: 我出石头！
t_en: I picked rock!

t_ch: 我出剪刀。
t_en: I picked scissors.

t_ch: 我出布~
t_en: I picked paper~

t_ch: 哎呀，又是平局。*我们*，谁都没法说服谁呢！
t_en: Oh no, another tie. We just can't convince each other!

t_ch: 就不能玩黑白配吗？
t_en: Can't you just settle it with heads or tails?

t_ch: 让我加入试试。
t_en: Let me join in and give it a try.

t_ch: 小灰，来得正好！*我们*有问题，想听听你的判断！
t_en: Little Gray, perfect timing! We were just hoping to hear your take!

t_ch: 餐馆现在的活动主题是「甜蜜蜜节」。可是，什么才是「甜」？
t_en: The restaurant's event theme is "Sweetie Festival." But here's the question: What exactly is "sweet"?

t_ch: 缇安觉得，这个问题很简单！含糖分多的食物，就是甜的！
t_en: It's super simple! Anything with tons of sugar counts as sweet!

t_ch: 所以朝面饼里，使劲地倒砂糖就对啦！
t_en: So we just need to pour a mountain of sugar into the dough!

t_ch: 缇宁不同意，甜是种复杂的感受。心情差的时候，吃什么都觉得没味道。
t_en: Trinnon disagrees. Sweetness is a more complicated feeling. If someone's in a bad mood, even the sweetest food might taste dull.

t_ch: 想营造甜蜜蜜的氛围，顾客的用餐体验很重要。
t_en: To really create that sweetie vibe, the overall dining experience has to be spot on.

t_ch: 唔，妈妈带回来的小甜饼，真的很好吃。缇宝提出办甜蜜蜜节，只是想重现这种味道。
t_en: Mmm... the little cookies Mom brought home really were yummy. Tribbie came up with Sweetie Festival because we wanted to recreate that kind of flavor.

t_ch: 但*我们*不知道，究竟要做什么呢？
t_en: But we still aren't sure what needs to be done...

t_ch: （同意缇安的观点）
t_en: (Agree with Trianne)

t_ch: 发现了吗？「缇安」与「甜」谐音，缇安的就是甜的，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Did you notice? "Trianne" has "tree" in her name, and she's reliable as an old oak, her hair's as red as a maple tree in autumn, and she's sweet as maple syrup!

t_ch: 小小灰，谢谢你。但这种事，根本不是理由啦！
t_en: Little Gray, thanks for the support. But that's not a valid reason!

t_ch: （支持缇宁的观点）
t_en: (Agree with Trinnon)

t_ch: 确实是这个理。高档餐厅里的菜品，感觉就是会更好吃。
t_en: You've got a point. Dishes at high-end restaurants do feel more delicious somehow.

t_ch: 缇宁在想，形成这个印象的，是环境吗，还是别的原因呢。
t_en: Trinnon's wondering... is that because of the environment? Or is it something else?

t_ch: （赞成缇宝的观点）
t_en: (Agree with Tribbie)

t_ch: 回忆里的总是最美好的，但人没法两次品尝同一份菜品啊。
t_en: Memories always taste the best, but no one can taste the exact same dish twice.

t_ch: 这个道理，缇宝也知道。究竟…该如何复现出回忆里的味道呢。
t_en: Tribbie understands that too. But still... how do you bring back the flavor of a memory?

t_ch: 既然争论不出结果，就先解散！都想想办法，*我们*一起把甜蜜蜜节办好！
t_en: Since we can't settle on a conclusion, let's take a break! Everyone, go brainstorm — we'll make Sweetie Festival a success together!

t_ch: 缇宝，你忘了件很重要的事。
t_en: Tribbie, you're forgetting something important.

t_ch: 甜蜜蜜节，可是，没有甜的材料！
t_en: A Sweetie Festival needs sweet ingredients!

t_ch: 瞧瞧我听到了什么。各位，莫非是遇到了物资短缺的麻烦？
t_en: Well, well, what do we have here? Could it be you're running into a little supply issue?

t_ch: 我这里应有尽有，树上长的，地面上跑的，都能给你们弄来。不打算瞧瞧嘛？
t_en: From fruits of the bough to beasts of the field — I've got everything. Care to take a look?

t_ch: 小飞儿，你也在这玩！*我们*想做很甜很甜的菜，你手头有适合的食材吗？
t_en: Little Ciphy, you're here too! We want to cook something suuuper sweet. Do you have the right ingredients?

t_ch: 有，当然有。几位客户，随便挑随便选，库存充足，包你们满意！
t_en: Oh, I've got plenty. Go ahead, browse all you want. My stock's full to the brim, satisfaction guaranteed!

t_ch: 只是，得多付钱。
t_en: Of course... it'll cost you.

t_ch: 坐地起价是吧？
t_en: Trying to jack up prices, are we?

t_ch: 哎，你可错怪我了。这地方与世隔绝，偏僻得很，运点东西进来老费劲了。
t_en: Ah, my friend, you wound me! This place is smack in the middle of nowhere. Hauling supplies here takes a lot of effort.

t_ch: 不能看熟人的面子打个折？
t_en: Can't you give us a discount, for old times' sake?

t_ch: 哎呦，真不行。这地方与世隔绝，偏僻得很，运点东西进来老费劲了。
t_en: Oof, no can do. This place is smack in the middle of nowhere. Hauling supplies here takes a lot of effort.

t_ch: 光说也没意思，先看看吧。但不准讨价还价！
t_en: Enough talk, see for yourself. But no haggling, got it?

t_ch: 做生意最讲究的是人脉，这个就送你们当见面礼了。
t_en: Connections are everything in business. Consider this your welcome gift.

t_ch: 好甜的蜂蜜！小飞儿的开价虽然不便宜，但味道很好呢。
t_en: Whoa, this honey's super sweet! Little Ciphy's prices might be steep, but the taste is totally worth it.

t_ch: 当然。这些货都是我拿宝贝换来的，质量绝对上乘，喜欢请再来！
t_en: Of course, these goods cost me a fortune in treasures. All of top-notch quality, I swear! Come back anytime, my friend~

t_ch: 反正这地方没有第二家店，不喜欢也没得选~
t_en: Not like you've got another shop to go to anyway~

t_ch: 我要向村长举报你！
t_en: I'm telling the village chief on you!

t_ch: 那句话怎么说的…现在是知识付费的时代，想知道新信息，就得掏钱。
t_en: Hey, how does the saying go...? "Knowledge ain't free these days." You want the good stuff, you gotta pay up.

t_ch: 搞垄断是要被抓起来的！
t_en: Monopolies are illegal!

t_ch: 嗐，要真说垄断，你们餐馆也不遑多让，哪有资格批评我呀。
t_en: Oh, if we're talking monopolies, your restaurant is just as guilty. So stop judging.

t_ch: 对了。听小动物说，你们餐馆办得像模像样的，有没有什么好吃的，让我也尝尝？
t_en: Anyway, the little critters say your restaurant is quite something. Got anything tasty for me to try too?

t_ch: 想吃餐馆的菜，就得付钱哦。就算是小飞儿也不能例外。
t_en: You want food from the restaurant, you gotta pay. Even you don't get a free pass, Little Ciphy.

t_ch: 缇宝阿姐，小气~
t_en: Aw~ Don't be so stingy, big sis Tribbie~

t_ch: 哼，好吧好吧，就算不给免费品尝，我自然有办法。
t_en: Hmph, fine. Even if I don't get to taste for free, I've got my own ways.

t_ch: 商品的订购方式已经发给你们了，欢迎随时光顾。回见！
t_en: I've also sent you details of my purchase methods. Buy whatever you need, anytime. Later!

t_ch: 小飞儿还是这么任性呢。不过买到了蜂蜜，可以做好多甜甜的菜品了。
t_en: Little Ciphy hasn't changed a bit. Still, now that we've got honey, we can whip up a whole slew of sweet dishes!

t_ch: 小灰，*我们*这就开始营业！
t_en: Little Gray, let's open for business!

t_ch: 今天就是评审的日子啦。缇安、缇宁，你们准备好了吗？
t_en: Today's the big day. The judges are coming! Trianne, Trinnon, are you ready?

t_ch: 缇安，朝面团里，倒了大罐的蜂蜜！
t_en: Trianne poured an entire jar of honey into the dough!

t_ch: 缇宁翻阅了成堆的资料，整理出好多让菜品变得更美味的小技巧。
t_en: Trinnon read a mountain of books and compiled tons of tips for making food taste even better.

t_ch: 那缇宝干了啥？
t_en: And what about Tribbie?

t_ch: 还有个在偷懒是吧？
t_en: Someone's slacking off, huh?

t_ch: 缇安，看到了。缇宝，一直在农田里玩！
t_en: Trianne saw it. Tribbie was out playing in the fields all day!

t_ch: 没有啦。缇宝也在很认真地做准备，只是…想给阿蝶留个惊喜！
t_en: That's not true! Tribbie was working super hard too... Tribbie just wanted to prepare a surprise for Cas.

t_ch: 好啦。小灰已经起床了。开始今天的营业吧！
t_en: Alright, Little Gray's up and about. Let's get today's business started!

t_ch: 迷…感觉来这家餐馆吃饭，好舒服，像是回家了迷！
t_en: Mem... eating here feels so comfy~ Like coming home, mem!

t_ch: 甜蜜蜜节到今天就结束啦。感谢各位的光临！
t_en: Sweetie Festival wraps up for the day. Thanks for stopping by, everyone!

t_ch: 感谢！喜欢就多来！
t_en: Thank you! If you liked it, come back often!

t_ch: 有任何意见，都可以写信告诉*我们*。放在门口的小邮箱里就好，谢谢。
t_en: If you have any suggestions, write us a letter and drop it in the little mailbox by the door. Thanks!

t_ch: 甜蜜蜜节的顾客反馈都很好哦，全是夸我们的呢。
t_en: Sweetie Festival has garnered amazing feedback. Everyone's been raving about us.

t_ch: 缇宝、缇安还有缇宁，三位活动负责人辛苦啦！
t_en: Big shoutout to Tribbie, Trianne, and Trinnon, our three event leads! Great job!

t_ch: 小妖精，喜欢就好！
t_en: Anything for our little fairies!

t_ch: 不过，现在还有重要的事。
t_en: But for now, we've got something important to take care of.

t_ch: 营业目标完成，就到美食评审会的时间啦。走吧，*我们*去把甜蜜蜜带给阿蝶！
t_en: Now that we've hit our sales target, it's time for the food review. Let us go bring some sweetness to Cas!

t_ch: 小灰，准备好去参加美食评审了嘛？
t_en: Little Gray, you ready to head to the food review?

t_ch: 出发咯！
t_en: Let's roll!

t_ch: 出发！大家一起去找阿蝶和小妖精玩咯！
t_en: Let's go! Time to find Cas and our little fairy friends!

t_ch: 还有点事要处理…
t_en: Still got a couple of things to wrap up...

t_ch: 不着急！*我们*就在这里等小灰！
t_en: No rush! We'll wait for you right here, Little Gray!

t_ch: 万众瞩目的迷其林美食评审，再度召开——
t_en: The long-awaited Memchelin Food Review is officially back —

t_ch: 近来，「{TEXTJOIN#225}」在迷路迷境中的关注度急剧上升，他们以美味的菜品与温暖贴心的服务，赢得了妖精们的极高赞誉。
t_en: Lately, "{TEXTJOIN#225}" has been gaining tons of buzz in the Membrance Maze. With delicious food and warm, thoughtful service, they've won over the hearts of our fairy folk.

t_ch: 近期举办的甜蜜蜜节更是广受好评，就连我也偷偷去光顾过。迷！千言万语汇聚成一句话…好吃！
t_en: The recently held Sweetie Festival was another big hit. Even I sneaked over for a bite. Mem! Words can't do it justice... It's just that good!

t_ch: 他们能否通过这次评审，几位专业的评审员又将给出怎样的评价呢？
t_en: Will they pass this latest round of judging? And what will the panel of expert reviewers have to say?

t_ch: ——拭目以待迷！
t_en: Let's see what'll happen!

t_ch: 小灰，有事想拜托你。这次的送审菜品，能让*我们*来决定嘛？
t_en: Little Gray, can we ask you a favor? Can we decide which dishes to submit for judging?

t_ch: 缇安想知道，谁做的菜更甜！
t_en: Trianne wants to know whose dish is sweeter!

t_ch: 正好有三位评审员，*我们*每人准备一道菜就好。
t_en: There happens to be three judges, so each of us only needs to prepare one dish.

t_ch: 行，我批准了。
t_en: Alright, you've got my approval.

t_ch: 你们可得好好干啊。
t_en: Better bring your A-game, everyone.

t_ch: 谢谢小灰！摆在那张空桌上对吧，*我们*这就端来！
t_en: Thanks, Little Gray! Just place them on that empty table, right? We'll go get everything ready right away!

t_ch: 迷迷？
t_en: Mem?

t_ch: 啊，耍赖！怎么和缇安，选了一样的菜！
t_en: Hey, that's not fair, picking the same dish as Trianne!

t_ch: 这就是所谓的心灵感应吧。
t_en: Guess this is what they call telepathy.

t_ch: 出现这个结果好像也正常？
t_en: I kinda feel this is to be expected?

t_ch: *我们*用各自的方式，对「甜蜜蜜节」做出了阐释。
t_en: We each had our own take on what the Sweetie Festival means.

t_ch: 虽然都是烤饼，但尝起来或许完全不同。
t_en: Even if it's all pancakes, the taste can still be totally different!

t_ch: 原来如此。为方便评审，能请三位老师分别讲讲自己的想法吗？
t_en: I see. To help the judges out, could each of you talk about your dish a little?

t_ch: 缇安，要先说！甜甜的菜，需要够甜。所以在饼里，加了好多蜂蜜。
t_en: Trianne first! Sweet food's gotta be super sweet! So Trianne packed that pancake with a ton of honey.

t_ch: 还有还有，不只这样，缇安偷偷找小敌，学了特别的技巧！
t_en: Oh, and also, Trianne secretly asked De to learn a special technique!

t_ch: 反复熬呀熬，让蜂蜜变得更甜，也不会腻。
t_en: Simmering it over and over till the honey turned extra rich and sweet, but not too heavy!

t_ch: 这就是，缇安的想法。甜蜜蜜的菜，要让尝的人甜到笑出来才行！
t_en: That sums up Trianne's idea! Sweet food should make people smile the moment they taste it!

t_ch: 缇安老师的选择是从菜品入手，改进烹饪技法。其余两位老师是怎么想的呢？
t_en: So Trianne focused on improving the ingredients and techniques. What about the other two chefs?

t_ch: 缇宁在书里看到过，优秀的餐馆会为顾客讲解菜品烹饪的相关知识，以便更好地品尝。
t_en: Trinnon read in a book that great restaurants explain to their guests how the food is made. It helps them enjoy it even more.

t_ch: 制作烤饼的小麦，是由餐馆的农林专家，经过反复拣选与实验，得到的优良品种。
t_en: The wheat used for these pancakes was carefully selected and cultivated by the restaurant's agricultural expert. Only the best made the cut after countless trials.

t_ch: 而我们的主厨，在揉制面团与最终烘焙时，也使用了悬锋城特有的技巧，能让烤饼的口感变得更为酥脆，同时令蜂蜜的香味得到最大程度的发挥。
t_en: And the head chef used a signature technique from Castrum Kremnos when kneading the dough and baking it, which makes the pancakes extra crisp and really brings out the full aroma of the honey.

t_ch: 不知道这样的菜品，是否合您的口味呢？
t_en: We wonder... does this flavor suit your palate?

t_ch: 有大饭店服务员的样子了！
t_en: Look at you, really starting to sound like a pro waiter at a fancy establishment!

t_ch: 没想到缇宁这么能吹…
t_en: Wow, didn't expect Trinnon to be this good at bragging...

t_ch: 小小灰，偷偷告诉你。缇宁，为了评审，背了好久的稿！
t_en: Little Gray, Trianne will let you in on a secret: Trinnon actually rehearsed that whole speech for days just for this!

t_ch: 看得出来，确实是用心制作的菜品。那么，缇宝老师准备的这份，又有什么独到之处？
t_en: You can tell a lot of love went into that dish. Now then, what's special about yours, Tribbie?

t_ch: 阿蝶，你尝尝就知道啦。
t_en: You'll know the moment you taste it, Cas.

t_ch: 我就不客气了。
t_en: Then I won't hold back!

t_ch: 除去饼壳的酥脆口感，与蜂蜜的甜意外，似乎还有别的…熟悉的味道。啊，这是……
t_en: Besides the crisp crust and the sweet honey, there's something else... something familiar. Wait, is this...

t_ch: 安提灵花的香气？
t_en: The scent of Antila flowers?

t_ch: 阿蝶尝出来啦。是的，烤饼里添加了安提灵的花瓣哦。
t_en: Cas figured it out! Yes. Tribbie added Antila petals to the pancake.

t_ch: 我觉得，甜蜜蜜的主题，最重要是让顾客感到幸福。但幸福的感觉，是因人而异的。
t_en: I feel the heart of the Sweetie Festival is making people feel happy. But happiness... means something different to everyone.

t_ch: 所以，餐馆在烹饪菜品时，也必须考虑到每位顾客的口味与喜好。
t_en: That's why a restaurant should tailor each dish to suit the guest's tastes and preferences.

t_ch: 这就是我专门为阿蝶特制的蜂蜜派！
t_en: So I made this honey pie just for you, Cas!

t_ch: …确实是回忆中的味道，非常有哀地里亚特色。谢谢。
t_en: ...Indeed, it tastes just like in my memories. Very much a taste of Aidonia. Thank you.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，缇宝、缇宁与缇安老师准备的菜品都是极其难得的美味，做出抉择相当困难。
t_en: There's no doubt that Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne each brought something amazing to the table. Choosing just one is not easy.

t_ch: 但作为餐馆的送审菜品，本次美食评审的结果应该很确定了。其余评审员觉得呢？
t_en: But since this dish is going to represent our restaurant, I think we've got a clear winner. What do the rest of the judges think?

t_ch: 迷！迷迷！
t_en: Mem! Mem, mem!!!

t_ch: 嗯。我也同意。
t_en: Agreed. That's what I thought.

t_ch: 还有位评审员似乎中途有事离开了。按照规则，我宣布，本次评审会的结果是——
t_en: Looks like one judge had to step out halfway. So by the rules, I'll go ahead and announce the winner of this culinary showdown—

t_ch: 等等！我还没投票呢！
t_en: Hold on! I haven't voted yet!

t_ch: 哎呀，刚才突然肚子疼，稍微离开了会，对不住啊各位。迷！
t_en: Sorry! I had a sudden stomachache and had to step out for a bit. My bad, everyone. Mem!

t_ch: 其余人都点评过了？我现在来尝尝。迷！
t_en: Has everyone else already given their verdict? Then let me grab a bite. Mem!

t_ch: 缇安，紧张起来了！
t_en: Uh-oh, Trianne's getting real nervous!

t_ch: 你们自己尝了吗？迷。
t_en: Have you all tried your own dishes? Mem.

t_ch: 尝了一块。
t_en: Yeah. One bite.

t_ch: 感觉怎么样？迷。
t_en: And? What'd you think? Mem.

t_ch: 味道很棒！这是*我们*精心准备的菜品，不会让各位失望的！
t_en: It was delicious! We really put our hearts into these dishes. You won't be disappointed!

t_ch: 嗯。我与另外那位评审员也给出了很高的评价。
t_en: Yes. The other judge and I both gave very high marks.

t_ch: 这么好吃？让我也尝尝。
t_en: That good, huh? Alright, let me have a taste too.

t_ch: ——呜哇！呸呸呸！
t_en: — WHOA!! Pfft! Pfft!!

t_ch: 到底是…是谁在菜里放了这么多糖啊？！就不知道猫不能吃甜食的吗！
t_en: Who dumped that much sugar in here!? Don't you know cats can't eat sweets!?

t_ch: 哇！小妖精，变成小小飞了！
t_en: Whoa! The little fairy just turned into a little Ciphy!

t_ch: 喵喵？不对，那群小动物的叫声是…迷迷？迷迷迷！
t_en: Mew? Wait, the sound of those little critters... Mem? Mem-mem-mem!

t_ch: 迷！迷迷迷迷，迷迷！
t_en: Mem! Memem, mememem!

t_ch: 好啊，你专程来偷吃的是吧？
t_en: Oh, I see what's going on. You showed up just to sneak some food, didn't you?

t_ch: 抓到了一只小馋猫！
t_en: Caught you, you little glutton!

t_ch: 嘿，我就说有办法能免费尝到吧。除了致死量的糖分以外，还挺好吃的。
t_en: Hey, told ya I had a way to score a free bite. Aside from the sugar overload, it's actually not half bad.

t_ch: 乐子有了，就不打扰各位啦。回见——
t_en: Well, that was fun. I'll leave you to it. Catch you later!

t_ch: 等等呀。赛飞儿小姐，有兴趣来餐馆工作吗？
t_en: Wait! Miss Cipher, have you ever considered working at our restaurant?

t_ch: 别。可别想拉我去你们那个餐馆打工，工作是不可能工作的，这辈子都不可能！
t_en: Nope. Don't even try to rope me into working in your restaurant, or even holding a job. Not happening. Not now, not ever!

t_ch: 现在没人管着我，自由自在，想干啥就干啥，我可太爱这种日子了。
t_en: No one's bossing me around these days. I do what I want, when I want, and I'm loving every second of it.

t_ch: 有缘再会！没缘分的话，有生意做也成啦。再见！
t_en: Until we meet again! If it's not the case, I'm always open to business. See ya!

t_ch: 虽然出了些小插曲，但赛飞儿阁下也对菜品给出了很高的评价。
t_en: Well, despite the little detour, Lady Cipher did give the dish high praise as well.

t_ch: 那么，我继续宣布本次美食评审的结果。{NICKNAME}阁下，请收下这枚奖章。
t_en: Now then, let's wrap this up. {NICKNAME}, please accept this medal.

t_ch: 迷！恭喜恭喜迷！「{TEXTJOIN#225}」通过了本次美食评审，是二星级餐馆了迷！
t_en: Mem! Congratulations! "{TEXTJOIN#225}" has passed the culinary review! You're now officially a 2-star restaurant! Mem!

t_ch: 继续加油！朝着四星级餐馆的目标前进迷！
t_en: Keep it up and shoot for that 4-star rating next!

t_ch: 太好了！
t_en: Hooray!

t_ch: 甜蜜蜜节圆满结束，餐馆经营也越来越顺利。真是充实的一天呢。
t_en: And with that, the Sweetie Festival comes to a perfect close. The restaurant's doing better than ever. What a fulfilling day.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}忙碌了这么久，也辛苦啦。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you've been working nonstop. Thank you for your hard work!

t_ch: 确实感觉累了。
t_en: Yeah, guess it does make me a bit tired.

t_ch: 累了就躺到床上，然后做个美美的梦。好好休息，明天见！
t_en: Then hit the hay and have a sweet, sweet dream. Rest up, and we'll see you tomorrow!

t_ch: 还有事情要处理…
t_en: Still got a couple of things to wrap up...

t_ch: 没关系，快去吧，我就在这等你哦。
t_en: No worries. Go ahead, I'll wait for you right here.

t_ch: 呼…装修完成。大家来看看餐馆的新门面吧。
t_en: Phew... Renovations are finally done. Come check out the restaurant's new look!

t_ch: 真厉害！现在餐馆真的有大饭店的模样啦。
t_en: Wow! It really looks like a top-tier establishment now!

t_ch: 白厄这么积极，说不定在厨师以外，你也有经理人的天赋哦？
t_en: With that kind of drive, Phainon, you might have a future as a manager too, not just a chef!

t_ch: 哈哈，那还真是幸运。能贡献更多，我求之不得。
t_en: Haha, wouldn't that be lucky! I'd love to help out in any way I can.

t_ch: 真可靠呀。既然大家都到齐了，就照例召开经营会议吧。有没有哪位自告奋勇，想要先发言的呢？
t_en: Guess we can always depend on you! Now, since everyone is here, let's kick off the weekly operations meeting. Anyone want to start?

t_ch: *我们*有新发现！甜蜜蜜节过后，缇安找小妖精们收集了用餐反馈。有条建议值得关注。
t_en: We found something! After the Sweetie Festival, Trianne gathered feedback from the little fairies, and there was this one comment worth noticing.

t_ch: 「迷！过来只是吃饭，太没意思！需要情绪价值！能让我开心的话，可以多给点钱迷！」
t_en: "Just coming here to eat is sooo boring! I need emotional value! Make me happy and I'll tip more! Mem!"

t_ch: 村里的小酒馆，有时候会安排吟游诗人讲述沿海百邦的奇妙见闻。或许，我们也可以采用类似的方法。
t_en: The tavern in the village sometimes hosts bards who share tales from the Seaside States. Maybe we could try something like that too?
